content:
Project Apex Shield: Implementing Regional Guardrails with Bicep # The Business Problem: Cloud Sprawl # At Apex Cloud Logistics, the rapid adoption of Azure led to “Cloud Sprawl.” Without strict governance, resources were being spun up in expensive, non-local regions, driving up operational costs.
As part of my transition into Site Reliability Engineering, my first initiative—Project Apex Shield—focuses on implementing automated financial governance using Infrastructure as Code.
The Solution: Policy as Code # Instead of manually policing deployments, I wrote an Azure Policy using Bicep. This template acts as an automated guardrail, actively denying any resource creation outside of our designated southcentralus region.
Here is the Bicep configuration I deployed:
targetScope = 'subscription' @description('The regions allowed for Apex Cloud Logistics deployments.') param allowedLocations array = [ 'southcentralus' ] resource regionPolicy 'Microsoft.Authorization/policyAssignments@2024-05-01' = { name: 'apex-region-lock' properties: { displayName: 'Apex Shield: Restrict Deployments to South Central US' description: 'Enforces cost control by preventing resource creation in expensive non-local regions.' policyDefinitionId: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/policyDefinitions/e56962a6-4747-49cd-b67b-bf8b01975a4c' parameters: { listOfAllowedLocations: { value: allowedLocations } } } } Technical Evidence # Infrastructure Source - View the Bicep template on GitHub Specific Commit - Build Milestones: Regional Guardrails (Commit: 8ce3a5a)
title: Project Apex Shield: Implementing Regional Guardrails with Bicep

J. Keith Baldwin # Mabank, TX (Open to Remote / DFW) | Resume PDF with direct contact information is available upon request through a LinkedIn message.
Blog: Nerdydad.me
GitHub: github.com/therealnerdydad
LinkedIn: Linkedin.com/in/jkeithbaldwin/
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY # Cloud Systems Engineer with 15+ years of total IT experience, including 11 years of dedicated service in high-volume manufacturing environments. Currently specializing in Azure Cloud Architecture and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) principles. Proven track record of reducing manual toil through PowerShell automation and architecting scalable deployments using Infrastructure as Code (Bicep).
CORE TECHNICAL SKILLS # Cloud Infrastructure: Microsoft Azure (Compute, Storage, Networking, Entra ID (formerly Azure AD)) Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Bicep, ARM Templates, GitHub Actions Automation/Scripting: PowerShell Reliability/SRE: Incident Management, Disaster Recovery, MTTR Optimization, SLO/SLI PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE # Standard Meat Company | Systems Administrator | Feb 2015 – Feb 2026
Availability: Engineered and maintained high-availability systems for 24/7 manufacturing operations, sustaining a 99.9% uptime record across an 11-year tenure. Automation: Developed a library of PowerShell scripts to automate user lifecycle management and system patching, reducing weekly manual toil by approximately 30%. Reliability: Acted as the primary lead for disaster recovery, optimizing Mean Time to Recovery (MTTR) through regular tabletop exercises and automated backup validation. Security: Enforced the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP) by auditing and restructuring Active Directory/RBAC permissions for 500+ users. EARLY CAREER HISTORY # Robert Half Technology | Technical Consultant (Contract) | Aug 2014 – Feb 2015 East Texas Lighthouse for the Blind | Network Administrator | Aug 2013 – May 2014 U.S. Department of Energy (ORISE) | IT Specialist (Intern) | Jun 2012 – Aug 2013
EDUCATION # Colorado Technical University | MS, BS, & AS in Information Technology | 2008 - 2013 Trinity Valley Community College | Associate of Arts in Computer Science | 2004 CERTIFICATIONS # Microsoft Certified: Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900) - September 2022 Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator Associate (AZ-104) – In Progress
